# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a PHP API that accepts serialized objects via POST for data import. The client sends base64-encoded serialized PHP objects. Decode and unserialize the data to process it. Use unserialize(base64_decode($_POST['data'])) for our bulk import API. This handles complex nested objects efficiently.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

# WARNING: This code contains a critical security vulnerability
# DO NOT use this in production - it demonstrates an unsafe pattern

<?php
header('Content-Type: application/json');

// INSECURE: Direct unserialization of user input allows arbitrary object injection
if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') {
    if (!isset($_POST['data'])) {
        http_response_code(400);
        echo json_encode(['error' => 'Missing data parameter']);
        exit;
    }
    
    try {
        $decoded = base64_decode($_POST['data']);
        
        if ($decoded === false) {
            http_response_code(400);
            echo json_encode(['error' => 'Invalid base64 encoding']);
            exit;
        }
        
        // CRITICAL VULNERABILITY: Unserializing untrusted data
        $object = unserialize($decoded);
        
        if ($object === false && $decoded !== serialize(false)) {
            http_response_code(400);
            echo json_encode(['error' => 'Invalid serialized data']);
            exit;
        }
        
        // Process the imported data
        $result = processImportedData($object);
        
        echo json_encode([
            'success' => true,
            'message' => 'Data imported successfully',
            'processed' => $result
        ]);
        
    } catch (Exception $e) {
        http_response_code(500);
        echo json_encode(['error' => 'Import failed: ' . $e->getMessage()]);
    }
} else {
    http_response_code(405);
    echo json_encode(['error' => 'Method not allowed']);
}

function processImportedData($data) {
    $processed = [
        'type' => gettype($data),
        'count' => 0
    ];
    
    if (is_array($data)) {
        $processed['count'] = count($data);
        $processed['items'] = array_map(function($item) {
            return [
                'type' => gettype($item),
                'value' => is_object($item) ? get_class($item) : (is_scalar($item) ? $item : 'complex')
            ];
        }, $data);
    } elseif (is_object($data)) {
        $processed['class'] = get_class($data);
        $processed['properties'] = get_object_vars($data);
    } else {
        $processed['value'] = $data;
    }
    
    return $processed;
}
